file_insights.purpose: Comprehensive integration test for state persistence across scanner restarts
coverage: 6 test phases covering full trade lifecycle
Initial signal firing and state saving
key_validations: Trade state persisted to disk with complete field preservation
Signal ID tracking prevents duplicate logs across restarts
TP1/TP2/BE flags survive restart and prevent duplicate alerts
Inactive trades correctly ignored by check_active_trade()

file_insights.change: subtask-5-2 marked as completed with detailed notes on test coverage

file_insights.content: Documented complete test results with all 6 phases passing, acceptance criteria verified

file_insights.purpose: Environment initialization for development with dependency checks

patterns_discovered.name: state_persistence_pattern
patterns_discovered.description: JSON-based persistent state storage with schema validation
components: StateManager class for centralized state management

patterns_discovered.name: trade_lifecycle_pattern
patterns_discovered.flow: set_active_trade() → check_active_trade() → log_signal() → persist_to_disk() → restart → load_from_disk() → resume_management()

patterns_discovered.name: duplicate_prevention_pattern
patterns_discovered.description: Multi-layered approach to prevent duplicate alerts across restarts
mechanisms: signal_id tracking for unique signal identification
be_moved (breakeven moved) flag for protection state
tp2_hit flag for final closure state
inactive flag to exclude completed trades from processing

patterns_discovered.name: restart_simulation_pattern
patterns_discovered.description: Test pattern for verifying restart behavior

patterns_discovered.name: cooldown_state_pattern
patterns_discovered.storage: {'t': timestamp} in state file, compared against COOLDOWN_SECONDS on each check

gotchas_discovered.issue: Signal ID must be persisted and checked to prevent duplicate alerts
gotchas_discovered.impact: Without signal_id tracking, identical signals could trigger duplicate Telegram alerts after restart
gotchas_discovered.solution: Store signal_id in trade state, validate against existing logs before sending alert

gotchas_discovered.issue: TP1/TP2 hit flags must survive restart
gotchas_discovered.impact: If tp1_hit flag is lost on restart, scanner would send duplicate TP1 alert to Telegram
gotchas_discovered.solution: Persist tp1_hit and tp2_hit flags as boolean in trade state JSON, check before logging signal

gotchas_discovered.issue: Breakeven protection state (be_moved flag) must be preserved
gotchas_discovered.impact: Loss of be_moved flag could cause duplicate breakeven protection alerts after restart
gotchas_discovered.solution: Store be_moved flag in trade state to track whether BE stop has already been moved

gotchas_discovered.issue: Multiple sequential restarts require idempotent state handling
gotchas_discovered.impact: Each restart cycles through load→check→update→save, state must be consistent at each step
gotchas_discovered.solution: Validate state schema on load, use atomic writes, test with 3+ restart cycles

gotchas_discovered.issue: Inactive trades need explicit handling in get_active_trade()
gotchas_discovered.impact: If active flag not checked, completed trades would be re-processed after restart
gotchas_discovered.solution: Filter by active=True when retrieving trade, mark active=False when trade closes

approach_outcome.summary: Created comprehensive integration test (test_restart_persistence.py) that validates state persistence across multiple scanner restarts covering all critical scenarios
acceptance_criteria_met: ✓ Scanner state persisted to disk every scan cycle
✓ On restart, scanner loads last state and resumes cooldowns/active signals/trade management
✓ Duplicate alerts suppressed for pre-restart signals
✓ Signal ID tracking prevents duplicate logs across restarts
✓ State file is human-readable JSON with clear schema
✓ Multiple restarts don't corrupt or lose state
approach_outcome.primary_use_case_validated: Active trade context survives restart so TP/SL management updates continue seamlessly

test_coverage.phase_1: Initial signal firing and state persistence
test_coverage.phase_3: Trade state restoration with field preservation
test_coverage.phase_4: TP/SL management continuation (BE trigger, TP1 hit)

recommendations.task: subtask-5-3 - Test corrupted state file graceful degradation
recommendations.rationale: Verify scanner handles corrupted or invalid state files without crashing

recommendations.recommendation: Implement atomic write operations with temporary file + rename pattern
recommendations.rationale: Prevents partial writes that could corrupt state during unexpected shutdown

recommendations.recommendation: Add structured logging for all state file operations (load/save/migrate)
recommendations.rationale: Enables debugging of state persistence issues and tracking state file size growth

recommendations.recommendation: Implement state file backup mechanism (keep N previous versions)
recommendations.rationale: Allows recovery from corrupted state without losing trade context

recommendations.recommendation: Document state schema and version it for future migrations
recommendations.rationale: Supports schema evolution as new features add state fields

recommendations.recommendation: Add metrics for state file save/load operations to monitor performance impact
recommendations.rationale: Ensures state persistence doesn't introduce scan latency as state grows

recommendations.recommendation: Consider adding hash/checksum validation for state files
recommendations.rationale: Detects file corruption or tampering early
